source: Scherer, K.R. (2005). What are emotions? And how can they be measured? Social Science Information, 44(4), 693-727. Swiss Center for Affective Sciences, University of Geneva. https://www.unige.ch/cisa/gew/
note: The GEW measures emotional reactions to objects, events, and situations. In this context: how does the brand communication make the viewer feel? The 20 emotion families are the official validated set from the instrument.
usage: Select max 2 values that best describe the emotional reaction a viewer would have to this brand content. Focus on what the brand intends to evoke, not your own reaction.

neutral_ambiguous.interest: curiosity, engagement, wanting to know more
neutral_ambiguous.longing: nostalgia, yearning, desire for something absent
neutral_ambiguous.awe: wonder, being overwhelmed by greatness or beauty

for_image_generation: Translate selected emotions into visual atmosphere: e.g. 'admiration' → elevated perspective, premium materials, clean space; 'longing' → warm golden light, distance, soft focus
